content.problemStatement:
Chef has 222 numbers AAA and BBB (A<B)(A \lt B)(A<B).

Chef will perform some operations on AAA.

In the ithi^{th}ith operation:


Chef will add 111 to AAA if iii is odd.
Chef will add 222 to AAA if iii is even.
Chef can stop at any instant. Can Chef make AAA equal to BBB?


Input Format

The first line contains a single integer TTT — the number of test cases. Then the test cases follow.
The first and only line of each test case contains two space separated integers AAA and BBB.


Output Format
For each test case, output `YES` if Chef can make AAA and BBB equal, `NO` otherwise.


Note that the checker is case-insensitive. So, `YES`, `Yes`, `yEs` are all considered same.

sampleTestCases.explanation:
Explanation:
Test case 1: Chef may perform one operation to make AAA equal to BBB: 1→+121 \xrightarrow{+1} 21+1​2
Test case 2: 3→+14→+263 \xrightarrow{+1} 4 \xrightarrow{+2} 63+1​4+2​6
Test case 3: It can be shown that it is impossible to make AAA and BBB equal.
Test case 4: 10→+111→+213→+114→+216→+117→+219→+12010 \xrightarrow{+1} 11 \xrightarrow{+2} 13 \xrightarrow{+1} 14 \xrightarrow{+2} 16 \xrightarrow{+1} 17 \xrightarrow{+2} 19 \xrightarrow{+1} 2010+1​11+2​13+1​14+2​16+1​17+2​19+1​20
